component_identification.content: Components include trail board with neutral and player buildings, hazard and teepee tiles, station master tiles, worker discs (cowboys, craftsmen, engineers), cattle cards, objective cards, coins, player boards, train tracks, and city delivery spaces. Each player has a deck of cattle cards and conducts repeated trips to Kansas City for deliveries.

core_game_loop.content: Players move their cowboy along the trail a limited number of spaces, resolving the destination building/tile action. Along the trail they hire workers, buy cattle, build structures, remove hazards, or gain certificates. Reaching Kansas City triggers hand evaluation and cattle delivery to a city, moving your train and gaining income/points before resetting to start another run.

detailed_rules.content: Movement can skip but often benefits from planned stops on your engine buildings. Hand value at Kansas City is the sum of distinct cattle breeds, modified by certificates to meet higher city requirements. Delivering to farther cities yields better rewards but may incur penalties if train position lags behind destination. Engineers advance train, unlocking station master bonuses and avoiding delivery debt. Cowboys improve cattle market access and deck quality; craftsmen strengthen board control through buildings. Deck-thinning and draw manipulation improve consistent high-value deliveries. Hazards and bandits (edition-dependent modules) can tax movement or provide removal rewards. Objectives provide points but can become liabilities if unmet, making selective commitment important.

scoring_and_endgame.content: Game end triggers when enough objective cards are taken or worker market depletes (edition/rules dependent), then players finish round parity and score. Points come from delivered city tiles, buildings, station masters, hazards/teepees, objectives, and some board bonuses, minus penalties such as unremoved hazards or incomplete objectives. Highest VP wins.

beginner_strategy.content: Improve deck quality early by buying better cattle and trimming weak duplicates when possible. Keep train progress roughly aligned with delivery ambitions to avoid costly penalties. Choose a worker focus (cowboy/craftsman/engineer) but add enough of another type to prevent bottlenecks. Build at least a few useful personal buildings in high-traffic trail spots. Time Kansas City arrivals so your hand is strong; rushing deliveries with poor variety wastes tempo.
